claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child I'd like to check eligibility for.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that context. Great, so you have one child you'd like to check eligibility for.

Before I assess the rules, let me ask:

**What is your child's name?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Now I have a few questions to determine Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me start with the first one:

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful information.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good to know. Now I need to understand your residency situation:

**Are you a UK resident, and do you have the right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Just to confirm the residency requirements:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status or pre-settled status in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Let me gather a bit more information:

**Is Alex in full-time education, or is Alex working?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is only 8, so just in primary school - not working or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I have one more important question:

**Are you currently receiving any other benefits for Alex, such as Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, income-related Employment and Support Allowance, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any of those benefits.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility. Let me review the rules and make my determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide you with my determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements because:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you
- You are a British citizen living in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's upkeep
- Alex is not in hospital or care
- You're not claiming means-tested benefits that would restrict your eligibility

You should be able to apply for Child Benefit now. If you have any further questions or your circumstances change, please feel free to contact the Child Benefit Office for assistance.

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16; (2) You are responsible for Alex as you live with them; (3) You live in the UK (Manchester); (4) You are a British citizen, which satisfies the residency requirement; (5) Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation; (6) Alex lives with you and nobody else is claiming for Alex; (7) You are not receiving Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, income-related Employment and Support Allowance, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit which could affect eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. As a British citizen living in the UK who is responsible for an 8-year-old child living with you, and not receiving means-tested benefits that would restrict eligibility, you meet all the core requirements for Child Benefit."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16; (2) You are responsible for Alex as you live with them; (3) You live in the UK (Manchester); (4) You are a British citizen, which satisfies the residency requirement; (5) Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation; (6) Alex lives with you and nobody else is claiming for Alex; (7) You are not receiving Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, income-related Employment and Support Allowance, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit which could affect eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. As a British citizen living in the UK who is responsible for an 8-year-old child living with you, and not receiving means-tested benefits that would restrict eligibility, you meet all the core requirements for Child Benefit."
